summary: Joel Spolsky writes a promotional year-end post encouraging readers to improve their careers in the new year. He highlights three paths: applying for Fog Creek's Management Training Program (which combines work experience with a Columbia Masters degree), browsing interesting job listings on his jobs board, or posting openings if you already work at a great company. The post features a testimonial from a current trainee and lists several compelling companies hiring through the board. It doubles as an advertisement for Fog Creek's jobs board with a discount coupon for employers.
key_points: Fog Creek's Management Training Program combines paid work experience with a funded Masters degree in technology management from Columbia
The jobs board features diverse, mission-driven companies working on surgical robots, mobile media, virtual worlds, and education
Joel frames career improvement as a concrete, actionable resolution rather than an abstract aspiration
The post subtly reinforces Joel's standards for good workplaces by contrasting them with dysfunctional environments
money_quotes: I woke up in the middle of the night the day after I read about it, knowing I had to apply.

No matter what I do here — learning FogBugz to do sales, learning SQL Server to do customer support, or even reconciling the credit card bill — I feel empowered in a way I've never felt at another workplace.
Once I'm done with my Fog Creek training and my degree from Columbia, I'll be a superlatively hireable package.

If you know of companies that are noisy, fluorescent, cubicle farms working 90 hour weeks, where the CTO refuses to use source code control because he's afraid people will blame him for the horrible code he writes, please don't tell them about this offer.
key_insight: Career improvement is best pursued through workplaces that invest in employee growth and maintain high standards for working conditions.
